limitations: Depends on canonical pitch coordinates and known pitch width.
capabilities.name: ball_lateral_fraction
capabilities.purpose: Normalize ball distance from pitch centerline toward either touchline.

limitations: Requires a supplied observed line and exact anchor-correlated release/reception relative-position evidence.
Does not identify whether the supplied line is a first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, causation, or defensive intent.
capabilities.name: controlled_line_break_episode

parameters.description: Distance from the supplied line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

capabilities.purpose: Evaluate whether a controlled pass carried a receiver from not-yet-beyond a supplied observed line at release to beyond that same line at controlled reception.

limitations: Uses event data for candidate passes and tracking data for release/reception proof.
Does not infer tactical intention, pass optimality, or decision quality.
capabilities.name: controlled_pass_episode

parameters.description: Search window before the event anchor for passer control.
parameters.name: release_search_before_seconds

parameters.description: Search window after the event anchor for ball departure.
parameters.name: release_search_after_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum release-to-controlled-reception search window.
parameters.name: reception_search_seconds

parameters.description: Maximum ball-player distance for control evidence.
parameters.name: control_distance_m

parameters.description: Margin allowing the intended player to count as controlling near teammates.
parameters.name: nearest_teammate_margin_m

parameters.description: Minimum receiver-control dwell for controlled reception.
parameters.name: minimum_receiver_dwell_seconds

capabilities.purpose: Identify completed pass actions where event data supplies the candidate passer/receiver and tracking proves physical release plus controlled reception.

limitations: Models only an observed geometric defensive line under declared thresholds.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
capabilities.name: defensive_line_model

parameters.description: Minimum distance beyond the ball for a defender to count as goal-side.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Maximum normalized x-span of defenders in one observed line band.
parameters.name: line_band_width_m

parameters.description: Minimum defending outfield players required to form an observed line.
parameters.name: minimum_line_defenders

parameters.description: Anchor-record frame field used to evaluate the observed line.
parameters.name: anchor_frame_field

capabilities.purpose: Fit an observed geometric defensive line at supplied anchor frames from defending outfield player tracking positions.

limitations: Goalkeepers are excluded from the outfield centroid.
capabilities.name: defensive_outfield_centroid
capabilities.purpose: Measure defending outfield team centroid without goalkeeper bias.

limitations: Does not infer intent, optimality, or missed opportunity.
capabilities.name: outcome_classification

parameters.description: Outcome lookahead horizon in seconds.
parameters.name: horizon

capabilities.purpose: Classify the post-anchor outcome under the frozen M1 predicates.

capabilities.name: possession_segment
capabilities.purpose: Identify active-ball possession intervals for the perspective team.

limitations: Measures entry into relation destination geometry only.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
capabilities.name: relation_destination_entry

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for relation destination entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic relation-entry record IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic witness ordering when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

capabilities.purpose: Measure whether the ball enters the exact destination region of a relation episode inside a declared post-open window.

limitations: Trusted recipe wrapper; agent-authored plans should use relation_destination_entry.
Requires an upstream relation episode set.
No pass probability, optimality, decision-quality, intent, or missed-opportunity claim.
capabilities.name: relation_destination_entry_classification

parameters.description: Lookahead horizon for destination lane entry.
parameters.name: destination_entry_horizon_seconds

parameters.description: Seed used to derive deterministic experimental result IDs.
parameters.name: result_id_seed

parameters.description: Deterministic selection strategy when an anchor has multiple relation episodes.
parameters.name: episode_selection

capabilities.purpose: Classify whether the ball later enters the destination region of a previously evaluated relation episode.

limitations: Classifies only observed position relative to a supplied geometric line.
Does not identify a tactical first, second, midfield, or back line.
Does not prove a line break, tactical intention, pass optimality, or defensive intent.
capabilities.name: relative_position_to_line

parameters.description: Entity-reference field read from the entity anchor record.
parameters.name: entity_id_field

parameters.description: Frame field at which the entity position is evaluated.
parameters.name: entity_frame_field

parameters.description: Distance from the line treated as level rather than ahead or behind.
parameters.name: line_buffer_m

capabilities.purpose: Classify an entity position as ahead of, behind, or level with an observed line at a declared frame.

capabilities.name: signed_lateral_shift
capabilities.purpose: Measure defensive centroid movement toward the ball side.

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No receiver body-orientation model.
No offside model in V1 acceptance.
capabilities.name: geometric_progressive_corridor

parameters.description: Maximum relation search window after the anchor.
parameters.name: max_window_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum forward progression from source to target.
parameters.name: minimum_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: minimum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: maximum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
parameters.name: minimum_clearance_m

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
parameters.name: open_after_frames

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
parameters.name: close_after_frames

parameters.description: Minimum open duration retained in the relation output.
parameters.name: minimum_duration_seconds

parameters.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.
parameters.name: side_filter

capabilities.purpose: Represent a geometrically clear forward connection from the ball location to an attacking teammate over an interval.

limitations: No pass probability.
No optimality or decision-quality claim.
No player intent, causation, or missed-opportunity claim.
No receiver body-orientation or offside model.
capabilities.name: geometric_progressive_corridor_from_anchor_set

parameters.description: Maximum relation search window after the anchor.
parameters.name: max_window_seconds

parameters.description: Minimum forward progression from source to target.
parameters.name: minimum_progression_m

parameters.description: Minimum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: minimum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Maximum source-target segment length.
parameters.name: maximum_segment_length_m

parameters.description: Minimum defender clearance from the source-target segment.
parameters.name: minimum_clearance_m

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered open.
parameters.name: open_after_frames

parameters.description: Frames required before a corridor is considered closed.
parameters.name: close_after_frames

parameters.description: Minimum open duration retained in the relation output.
parameters.name: minimum_duration_seconds

parameters.description: Retain corridors by relation destination side relative to the ball side.
parameters.name: side_filter

capabilities.purpose: Represent a geometrically clear forward connection from a generic anchor set to an attacking teammate over an interval.

limitations: Counts relative ball-position change between release and controlled reception.
Does not prove the pass alone caused every opponent to be bypassed.
Goalkeepers are excluded from the opponent denominator.
capabilities.name: opponents_bypassed_by_action

parameters.description: Release-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is goal-side of the ball.
parameters.name: goal_side_buffer_m

parameters.description: Reception-frame buffer for deciding whether an opponent is behind the ball.
parameters.name: bypassed_buffer_m

capabilities.purpose: Count opposition outfield players who were goal-side of the ball at action release and behind the ball at controlled reception.

operators.name: count_at_least
operators.purpose: Count a boolean or entity collection and compare to a count threshold.

operators.name: eq
operators.purpose: Compare a signal to an equal typed value.

operators.name: exists
operators.purpose: Test whether a boolean episode or anchor-evaluation collection exists.

operators.name: gt
operators.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to a strict lower numeric threshold.

operators.name: gte
operators.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to a numeric threshold.

operators.name: lte
operators.purpose: Compare a numeric signal to an upper numeric threshold.

operators.name: neq
operators.purpose: Compare a signal to a non-equal typed value.

limitations: UNKNOWN remains UNKNOWN; absence is not converted to FALSE.
operators.name: persists_for
operators.purpose: Promote a boolean frame signal to intervals after minimum duration.

runtime_contexts.description: Canonical ball position signal in pitch coordinates.
runtime_contexts.name: canonical_match_state.ball_position

runtime_contexts.description: Canonical tracking samples around an event or anchor window.
runtime_contexts.name: canonical_tracking_window

runtime_contexts.description: Provider event candidates for completed passes.
runtime_contexts.name: provider_event_stream.completed_pass_events
